rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (two LGTM approvals plus a manual verification comment confirming the fix works), so both candidates trivially satisfy recall and neither can score a gap_hit. X goes further: it validates the core diff correctly, then flags three specific stale hardcoded-'3' boundary tests in reliability suites outside the diff that would break from this exact change, with precise file:line and before/after fixes — a substantive, high-value catch mirroring the pattern the PR author already fixed elsewhere, though these files aren't in the visible diff so their existence can't be fully re-verified here. Y's single finding (missing positive unit test for layers=2) is fully grounded in the visible diff and clearly valid, but is a minor nitpick already largely covered by the e2e test the PR modified. X's review is more thorough, equally well-grounded on the parts checkable against the diff, and more actionable overall.
